_comment: Hanover Park 'Chief of Staff' cut. Hanover Park = AI-enabled fund administration / fund-CFO software ($31M from Emergence, Susa, angel fund CFOs). CoS to the CEO: run the operating system, turn founder priorities into owners/deadlines, own the company financial model, build internal operating infrastructure (dashboards/trackers/live priority views), cross-functional follow-through, special projects end-to-end. Ideal background per JD: ex-founder, CoS at a hypergrowth company, or ex-IB who wants to operate. So this variant reframes Adam as a CoS/OPERATOR (not an investor): LEADS with the 19days CoS/Venture-Ops role (operating system + owned financial models + fund ops from scratch + cross-functional execution) and his production internal-AI operating platform, then Aeon (ex-founder), then Atento for private-markets / fund-admin familiarity (a JD nice-to-have). Built by /tailor-resume. Never fabricate.
name: Adam Breaux
summary: Chief-of-staff operator who turns a founder's priorities into owners, deadlines, and things that actually land. Three years as Chief of Staff and Venture Operations Lead at a venture studio: built the operating system, dashboards, and reporting from scratch, owned financial models and fund operations end to end, and drove cross-functional follow-through across product, finance, sales, and people. Co-founder of an AI consulting practice and an early, expert adopter of frontier AI tooling ('AdamGPT') — builds the internal AI systems that keep fast-moving companies from losing things in the gaps. Fluent in private markets and fund administration from running LP reporting and diligence on a $100M+ fund. Context-switches across functions without losing the thread, and owns the resolution rather than just the analysis.

items.title: Chief of Staff to CEO / Venture Operations Lead
bullets: Connective tissue between the CEO's priorities and the org's execution: turned founder priorities into owners, deadlines, and execution plans, ran follow-through on cross-functional initiatives across product, finance, sales, recruiting, and ops, and caught operational loose ends before they escalated.

Built the internal operating system from scratch — defined the metrics that mattered at venture and studio level, stood up portfolio tracking, dashboards, and live reporting, and led the studio's annual LP meeting end to end (logistics through financials and projections).

Owned financial models end to end: built and maintained three-statement, returns, and fund models (computed TVPI for fundraising) and the financial model behind Agentech's $3M seed — investor memo, projections, and data room — to a fast, fully-subscribed close; ran the same process across multiple ventures (~$10M raised studio-wide). Serve as part-time CFO of Agentech (~$25M valuation).

Built internal AI operating infrastructure: a Claude-based operations platform spanning CRM, call transcripts, email, calendar, and contracts that auto-drafts morning briefs, meeting agendas, and proposals, plus a multi-agent sourcing engine with a continual-learning loop — the systems that keep a small team from dropping things in the gaps.

0-to-1 special projects: took Agentech from a blank page to a funded company — scoped, staffed, tracked, and closed it out — built the team, ran sales to the first $600K ARR, and stood up the studio's entire back office (HR, payroll, accounting) from zero.

items.title: Co-Founder
items.org: Aeon  —  bespoke AI products for industrial companies
bullets: Co-founded and operate an AI products practice — own executive discovery, scoping, staffing, delivery, and adoption for energy and manufacturing clients; the founder's seat, carrying real responsibility from day one under incomplete information and tight timelines.
Daily hands-on builder of production AI systems — fluent in exactly the kind of internal AI tooling an AI-native company runs on.

items.title: Venture Fellow / Investment Diligence
items.org: Atento Capital  —  VC arm of the George Kaiser Family Foundation ($100M+ fund)
bullets: Private-markets and fund fluency: ran fund-level diligence, bottom-up market sizing, competitive analysis, and financial-projection review delivered directly to a GP; sourced 14 of 20 finalists — the most of any of ~12 fellows.
On all three deals I diligenced, I recommended against — all three are no longer operating.

items.title: Founding President — starTUp (University of Tulsa VC Club)
bullets: Founded TU's first VC club from scratch and placed members as analysts on the 46VC Hurricane Venture Fund, a multi-million-dollar student/alumni fund.

items.title: Chair, Macroeconomic Committee — TU Student Investment Fund ($8M)
bullets: Built a new decision-making process still in use today; made buy/sell calls and led analysts to execute.

items.title: Master of Business Administration — Finance & Entrepreneurship
B.S. Exercise Science / Pre-Med
items.org: The University of Tulsa  —  Division I Cross Country & Track and Field

sections.heading: Skills & Strengths
items: Chief-of-staff execution: turning priorities into owners, deadlines, and follow-through
Financial modeling — build and own a model, not just read one (three-statement, returns, fund)
Building internal operating infrastructure from scratch (dashboards, trackers, reporting)
Cross-functional context-switching across finance, ops, product, and people
Private markets & fund administration; LP reporting; $100M+ fund diligence
Frontier-AI fluency; building production internal AI / agentic systems ('AdamGPT')
High ownership; bias to action under incomplete information and tight timelines
